hypotheses.question: Is the ibm_fez elevated null YX floor (fez nulls 0,2,4: |z|=4.89,2.30,4.40, argmax YX) device-intrinsic coherent multitime structure or a drift fluke?

physical_qubits_expected.note: VF2 deterministic layout; verified in-run per instance

hypotheses.H_intrinsic: null YX reproduces at |z|>3.6 on >=4 of 6 instances with consistent sign across the shared physical-qubit triple; equivalently pooled 12288-shot YX mean nonzero at high z with agreeing per-instance signs
hypotheses.H_drift: excesses do not reproduce: |z|<3.6 on nearly all (<=1 of 6) instances, scattered signs, and/or first-half vs second-half split z crossing the gate within a single instance
hypotheses.H_inconclusive: 2-3 of 6 exceed, or majority exceed but signs inconsistent, or strong within-run half-split instability
hypotheses.half_split: each 2048-shot PUB split 1024/1024 in shot order -> split z

qpu_seconds_estimate.note: empirical fez usage ran ~1.5x this high estimate; 2048-shot plan stays comfortably under 60 QPU-s
